architecture.summary: The system extracts interpretable concepts from autoregressive music generator transformers by applying sparse autoencoders (SAEs) to the residual stream. Discovered concepts are automatically labeled and validated using interpretability pipelines. Concepts identified as steering-relevant are then used to steer model generations by intervening on the latent representations.
trained_components: sparse autoencoders applied to residual stream

biblio.title: Discovering and Steering Interpretable Concepts in Large Generative Music Models

claims.statement: Internal representations of autoregressive music generators align with traditional musical constructs such as chord progressions
SAEs discover coherent but uncodified musical patterns lacking clear counterparts in music theory or language
Discovered concepts can be used to steer model generations
The approach is made scalable and evaluable using automated labeling and validation pipelines
